concept.title: XSD Elements and Data Types
concept.shortdesc: XSD defines elements using simple types (for text-only content) and complex types (for elements with children or attributes) — with 44 built-in data types for precise validation.

section.title: Simple elements
section.p: A simple element contains only text — no child elements and no attributes. You declare it using a built-in type:
section.codeblock: <xs:element name="title" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="year" type="xs:integer"/> <xs:element name="price" type="xs:decimal"/> <xs:element name="published" type="xs:date"/> <xs:element name="inStock" type="xs:boolean"/>
section.p: These declarations say: <title> must contain a string, <year> must contain an integer, <price> must contain a decimal number, and so on. A document with <year>not-a-number</year> would fail validation.

section.title: Default and fixed values
section.p: Simple elements can have default or fixed values:
section.codeblock:
<!-- If omitted, defaults to "Unknown" --> <xs:element name="author" type="xs:string" default="Unknown"/>
<!-- Always this value — cannot be changed --> <xs:element name="version" type="xs:string" fixed="1.0"/>

section.title: Complex types: Elements with structure
section.p: An element that contains child elements, attributes, or both uses a complex type. There are several patterns:
Sequence — children must appear in the specified order:
section.codeblock: <xs:element name="book"> <xs:complexType> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="title" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="author" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="year" type="xs:integer"/> <xs:element name="price" type="xs:decimal"/> </xs:sequence> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
section.p: Choice — exactly one of the listed children can appear:
section.codeblock: <xs:element name="payment"> <xs:complexType> <xs:choice> <xs:element name="creditCard" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="bankTransfer" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="cash" type="xs:string"/> </xs:choice> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
section.p: All — children can appear in any order, each at most once:
section.codeblock: <xs:element name="address"> <xs:complexType> <xs:all> <xs:element name="street" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="city" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="zip" type="xs:string"/> </xs:all> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>

section.title: Occurrence constraints: minOccurs and maxOccurs
section.p: XSD provides precise control over how many times a child element can appear:
section.codeblock: <xs:element name="book"> <xs:complexType> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="title" type="xs:string"/> <!-- 1 to 5 authors --> <xs:element name="author" type="xs:string" minOccurs="1" maxOccurs="5"/> <!-- Optional subtitle --> <xs:element name="subtitle" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0"/> <!-- Unlimited keywords --> <xs:element name="keyword" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0" maxOccurs="unbounded"/> </xs:sequence> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
section.p: Compare this to DTD, which can only express "exactly one", "optional" (?), "one or more" (+), and "zero or more" (*).

section.title: Defining attributes in XSD
section.p: Attributes are declared inside complex types using <xs:attribute>:
section.codeblock: <xs:element name="book"> <xs:complexType> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="title" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="author" type="xs:string"/> </xs:sequence> <xs:attribute name="isbn" type="xs:string" use="required"/> <xs:attribute name="format" type="xs:string" default="paperback"/> <xs:attribute name="language" type="xs:string" use="optional"/> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>

ul.li: use="prohibited" — must not appear (useful when restricting inherited types)

section.title: Named types: Reusing type definitions
section.p: Instead of defining types inline (anonymous), you can name them for reuse:
section.codeblock:
<xs:schema xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">
<!-- Named complex type --> <xs:complexType name="addressType"> <xs:sequence> <xs:element name="street" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="city" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="zip" type="xs:string"/> <xs:element name="country" type="xs:string"/> </xs:sequence> </xs:complexType>
<!-- Reuse in multiple elements --> <xs:element name="shippingAddress" type="addressType"/> <xs:element name="billingAddress" type="addressType"/>
</xs:schema>
